title.en: The EASIEST way to make Korean friends! (Chiming in)
title.ko: 저도 그거 진짜 좋아해요! (상대방의 취향에 맞장구치기)

description.en: Stop being just a listener! Master the art of active agreement and use phrases like "저도 그거 진짜 좋아해요" to build instant connections.
description.ko: 처음 만난 사람과 1분 만에 친해지는 비결! 상대방의 취향이나 취미에 격하게 공감하며 대화의 에너지를 높이는 맞장구 리액션을 배워봅니다.

tags: Situation. Social. Hobby. Agreement. Empathy. Friendship. Season 2

content.speaker: John
content.text: Mina, I’ve realized that the best way to make friends in Korea is finding something in common. But just nodding my head feels a bit boring. How can I show that I’m really into what they’re talking about?

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 맞아요! 한국 사람들은 대화할 때 리액션이 좋은 사람에게 금방 마음을 열거든요.
content.translation: Right! Koreans quickly open their hearts to people who have great reactions during a conversation.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 상대방이 좋아하는 걸 나도 좋아한다고 말할 때 조금 더 에너지를 실어서 말하는 게 포인트예요.
content.translation: The point is to speak with a bit more energy when saying that you also like what the other person likes.

content.speaker: John
content.text: So, instead of just saying "Me too," I should use a more active phrase?

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 네! 그럴 때 가장 좋은 리액션이 바로..., "저도 그거 진짜 좋아해요!" 라고 말하는 거예요.
content.translation: Yes! The best reaction for that is "저도 그거 진짜 좋아해요!".

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 오늘은 대화에 활기를 불어넣는 공감 리액션을 배워볼게요!
content.translation: Today, we'll learn empathy reactions that breathe life into conversations!

content.speaker: John
content.text: 저도 그거 진짜 좋아해요. I can use this for movies, food, or any hobby, right?

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 그럼요! "나도 당신과 취향이 같다" 라는 사실을 아주 기분 좋게 전달하는 표현이에요.
content.translation: Of course! It's an expression that very pleasantly conveys the fact that "I have the same taste as you."

insight.title: 저도 그거 진짜 좋아해요
insight.usage_tip: '저도(Me too)' + '그거(That)' + '진짜(Really)' + '좋아해요(Like)'. Adding '진짜' makes your interest sound much more sincere.

content.speaker: John
content.text: 저도 그거 진짜 좋아해요!
content.translation: Wow, I really like that too!

content.speaker: John
content.text: What if I want to agree with their opinion, not just their taste?

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 그럴 때는. "제 말이 그 말이에요!" 라고 해보세요. "내가 하고 싶었던 말이 바로 그거다" 라는 뜻으로 격한 공감을 나타낼 때 최고예요.
content.translation: In that case, try saying "제 말이 그 말이에요!". It means "That's exactly what I wanted to say," and it's the best for showing strong agreement.

insight.title: 제 말이 그 말이에요
insight.usage_tip: '제 말(My words)' + '이(Is)' + '그 말(Those words)' + '이에요(Is)'. Use this when someone points out something you've been thinking.

content.speaker: John
content.text: Okay, let's jump in. We're at a cafe, and we just started talking about our favorite Korean foods.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 저는 매운 음식을 정말 좋아해요! 특히 스트레스 풀 때는 떡볶이가 최고인 것 같아요.
content.translation: John, I really love spicy food! Especially when I'm stressed, I think Tteokbokki is the best.

content.speaker: John
content.text: 진짜요? 저도 그거 진짜 좋아해요! 매운 거 먹으면 기분이 좋아지잖아요.
content.translation: Wow, really? I really like that too! Eating spicy food makes you feel better, right?

insight.title: 저도 그거 진짜 좋아해요
insight.usage_tip: Using a high tone of voice here helps match the other person's enthusiasm.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 맞아요! 그런데 요즘 떡볶이는 너무 매워서 가끔 힘들더라고요.
content.translation: Right! But lately, Tteokbokki is so spicy that it's hard sometimes.

content.speaker: John
content.text: 제 말이 그 말이에요! 맛있게 매워야 하는데 가끔 너무 맵기만 해서 아쉬워요.
content.translation: That's exactly what I'm saying! It should be deliciously spicy, but sometimes it's just too spicy, so it's a shame.

insight.title: 제 말이 그 말이에요
insight.usage_tip: This shows you aren't just listening, but you deeply understand their point of view.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 존이랑 저랑 대화가 정말 잘 통하는데요? 우리 다음에 떡볶이 맛집 같이 가요!
content.translation: Oh, John, we really communicate well! Let's go to a famous Tteokbokki place together next time!

content.speaker: John
content.text: I can see why people say reactions are the key to friendship. Mina and I became "spicy food buddies" in a minute!

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 맞아요. 상대방의 말에 기분 좋게 맞장구치는 것만으로도 아주 멋진 친구를 사귈 수 있답니다.
content.translation: Right. Just by pleasantly chiming in with the other person's words, you can make a very wonderful friend.

content.speaker: John
content.text: Let's review our "chiming in" reactions one last time.

content.speaker: John
content.text: 저도 그거 진짜 좋아해요!
content.translation: I really like that too!

content.speaker: John
content.text: 제 말이 그 말이에요!
content.translation: That's exactly what I'm saying!

content.speaker: John
content.text: 진짜요? 저랑 똑같네요!
content.translation: Wow, really? You're just like me!

content.speaker: John
content.text: 저도 그렇게 생각해요!
content.translation: I think so too!

content.speaker: John
content.text: 역시 대화가 잘 통하네요!
content.translation: As expected, we communicate well!

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 여러분도 오늘 누군가와 대화할 때 마음을 다해 맞장구쳐 보세요. "저도 그거 진짜 좋아해요!"
content.translation: Everyone, when you talk to someone today, try chiming in with all your heart. "I really like that too!"

content.speaker: John
content.text: That's all for today. See you in the next episode.

content.speaker: John
content.text: 대박! This was so helpful.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 다음에 또 만나요. 안녕~!
content.translation: See you again next time. Bye~!

vocabulary.example: 친구의 말에 맞장구를 잘 쳐주면 대화가 끊이지 않아요. (If you chime in well with your friend's words, the conversation won't stop.)
사람마다 옷을 입는 취향이 다 달라요. (Everyone has different tastes in clothing.)
상대방의 슬픈 사연에 깊이 공감했어요. (I deeply empathized with the other person's sad story.)

vocabulary.meaning: To communicate well / To be understood
vocabulary.example: 우리는 처음 만났지만 대화가 정말 잘 통했어요. (We met for the first time, but we communicated really well.)
미나 씨는 항상 밝은 에너지를 가지고 있어요. (Mina always has bright energy.)
시장에 가니까 사람들의 활기가 느껴져서 좋네요. (Going to the market, it's nice to feel the people's vitality.)
